# LiveFolders

> Chain tools into one call. Declare a pipeline in `folder.yaml` — the agent triggers the whole chain with a single `echo` / `cat`. No per-step JSON, no per-step model turn. 1 round trip per workflow, not N. Built in Rust, zero dependencies for tool authors.

LiveFolders mounts a virtual filesystem on your machine (via FUSE). Every installed tool appears as a directory of plain files. An LLM reads a file to call the tool — no JSON protocol, no SDK, no special client. Any agent that can run `cat` or `echo` can use it. Multi-step workflows are declared as pipelines in `folder.yaml` and run as a single round trip.

## Why a folder beats JSON tool-calling

- **1 round trip per workflow, not N.** A 5-step chain is one model turn, not five. The agent writes once and reads once; the kernel runs the pipeline.
- **No schema chatter between stages.** Stages hand bytes to each other on stdin/stdout — no re-serializing through the model, no per-call validation overhead.
- **Lazy discovery.** `ls`, `cat index.md`, `cat how_to.md`. Tool docs load only when the agent needs them, instead of every schema squatting in the context window.
- **Shell composition for free.** Agents already know `|`, `xargs`, `tee`. Composing tools doesn't require a new protocol.

## Pipelines in one folder.yaml

```yaml
# tools/weather/folder.yaml
files:
  - name: report
    type: write_invoke
    pipe: [fetch_data, translate, format]   # 3 steps, 1 endpoint
```

```bash
echo "London" > .livefolders/tools/weather/report
cat  .livefolders/tools/weather/report
# → fetch → translate → format, all in one round trip
```

- Version: 0.22.0
- License: Apache 2.0
- GitHub: https://github.com/natanloterio/LiveFolders
- Website: https://www.livefoldersfs.org

## How It Works

Mount the filesystem with `livefolders mount`. Tools appear under `.livefolders/tools/`. Each tool has:

- `how_to.md` — human-readable usage instructions (LLM reads this first)
- `schema.json` — machine-readable schema (mirrors MCP's `list_tools` format)
- `index.md` — lists all installed tools

To invoke a tool: write input with `echo`, read output with `cat`. The write call blocks until the handler finishes.

```
cat .livefolders/tools/users/list          # read-invoke: fetches users from API
echo "London" > .livefolders/tools/weather/forecast  # write-invoke: sends query
cat .livefolders/tools/weather/forecast    # read result
cat .livefolders/tools/weather/forecast.log  # timing + stderr from last run
```

## Tool Definition (folder.yaml)

Tools are defined in 6–10 lines of YAML — no server process, no imports:

```yaml
name: users
description: List users from the mock API.
files:
  - name: list
    type: read_invoke
    handler: >-
      curl -s https://mockapi.io/users
      | jq -r '"# Users\n", (.[] | "## \(.name)\nID: \(.id)\n")'
  - name: how_to.md
    type: readonly
```

## Key Features

- **Hot-Reload**: Edit `folder.yaml` or a handler script — changes take effect in ~1 second, no restart
- **Sandbox Isolation**: Landlock LSM + seccomp (Linux), sandbox-exec (macOS); network blocked by default; `network: true` to opt in; `mode: strict` refuses to mount if isolation unavailable
- **Concurrent Shell Sessions** (v0.22.0): Invocation buffers scoped by `(inode, session-id)` — two pipelines from different shells no longer clobber each other's results. Handlers dispatched async off the FUSE thread so parallel pipelines actually run in parallel. Abandoned slots reaped after 15-minute idle TTL.
- **Input Validation**: Per-endpoint `min_length`, `max_length`, `pattern`, JSON `schema:` block — validated before handler runs, auto-documented in `how_to.md`
- **Structured Errors**: All failures return `[ERROR:CODE] message` (5 parseable codes)
- **Logs**: Each endpoint exposes `<endpoint>.log` with `duration_ms` and `stderr` from last run
- **Stateful Tools**: Declare `state_file:` in `folder.yaml`; runtime holds exclusive `flock` for each call, injects `LIVEFOLDERS_STATE_FILE`
- **Declarative Pipelines**: `pipe: [stage1, stage2]` chains endpoints — stdout→stdin in one LLM write, per-stage validation
- **Schema Discovery**: `schema.json` mirrors MCP's `list_tools` format — machine-readable by MCP-aware clients
- **Handler Timeouts & SIGKILL Escalation**: Hung tools receive SIGTERM at configurable timeout (default 30s), then SIGKILL after 1s grace if still running; returns error string
- **Secret Management**: Declare required env vars in `folder.yaml`; users prompted at install; stored in `secrets.env`

## Agent Auto-Setup

`livefolders init` detects the active agent and writes the instruction snippet automatically:

| Agent | File |
|-------|------|
| Claude Code | `CLAUDE.md` |
| Cursor / Windsurf / Aider | `AGENTS.md` |
| GitHub Copilot | `.github/copilot-instructions.md` |

## Install

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/natanloterio/LiveFolders/master/install.sh | bash
```

Prerequisites: `fuse3` (Linux) or macFUSE (macOS).

## vs MCP

LiveFolders is a POSIX-native alternative to MCP (Model Context Protocol). MCP is better for web clients and hosts with native MCP support. LiveFolders is better for shell-capable agents, local dev, and tool authors who want zero-dependency definitions.

Full comparison: https://github.com/natanloterio/LiveFolders/blob/master/docs/livefoldersfs-vs-mcp.md

## Research

"Files All the Way Down: A Design Space Analysis of Filesystem-Native LLM Tool Integration" — proposes a two-axis taxonomy (coupling depth × invocation interface), evaluates 7 systems across 10 criteria. Central finding: coupling depth predicts ergonomics ceiling; invocation interface predicts safety floor. LiveFolders scores ✓ on 7/10 criteria.

Paper: https://github.com/natanloterio/LiveFolders/tree/master/paper
